### Sign-In Success Rate

The proportion of sign-in attempts that succeeded, across all of CanadaLogin.

This metric is useful for monitoring overall authentication health. A sustained
drop can indicate a service problem, a misconfigured application, or elevated
attack traffic inflating the failure count.

*Alternative names*:

- authentication success rate
- login success rate
- overall sign-in success rate
- successful sign-in proportion

**Calculation.** Divide the sum of `successful_logins` by the sum of all attempts
(`successful_logins + failed_logins`) over the selected reporting period.

To track the metric over time, bin the same calculation by week. Truncating
`from_date` to the week groups each day into its Monday-anchored bin, so the
result is one success rate per Monday-to-Sunday week.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## SQL

Overall:

```sql
SELECT
    CAST(SUM(successful_logins) AS DOUBLE)
        / NULLIF(SUM(successful_logins + failed_logins), 0) AS sign_in_success_rate
FROM ibm_verify.auth_total_logins
-- set the reporting window; see the timespans note in cookbook/notes.qmd
WHERE from_date BETWEEN DATE '2026-01-01' AND DATE '2026-05-28';
```

By week:

```sql
SELECT
    date_trunc('week', from_date) AS week_start,
    CAST(SUM(successful_logins) AS DOUBLE)
        / NULLIF(SUM(successful_logins + failed_logins), 0) AS sign_in_success_rate
FROM ibm_verify.auth_total_logins
-- set the reporting window; see the timespans note in cookbook/notes.qmd
WHERE from_date BETWEEN DATE '2026-01-01' AND DATE '2026-05-28'
-- date_trunc('week', ...) anchors each bin to its Monday
GROUP BY date_trunc('week', from_date)
ORDER BY week_start;
```

## R

Overall:

```r
tbl(con, in_schema("ibm_verify", "auth_total_logins")) |>
  # set the reporting window; see the timespans note in cookbook/notes.qmd
  filter(
    from_date >= as.Date("2026-01-01"),
    from_date <= as.Date("2026-05-28")
  ) |>
  summarise(
    sign_in_success_rate =
      sum(as.double(successful_logins), na.rm = TRUE) /
        sum(successful_logins + failed_logins, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```

By week:

```r
tbl(con, in_schema("ibm_verify", "auth_total_logins")) |>
  # set the reporting window; see the timespans note in cookbook/notes.qmd
  filter(
    from_date >= as.Date("2026-01-01"),
    from_date <= as.Date("2026-05-28")
  ) |>
  # floor_date(..., "week") translates to Athena's date_trunc('week', ...),
  # which anchors each bin to its Monday
  mutate(week_start = floor_date(from_date, "week")) |>
  group_by(week_start) |>
  summarise(
    sign_in_success_rate =
      sum(as.double(successful_logins), na.rm = TRUE) /
        sum(successful_logins + failed_logins, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  arrange(week_start)
```

:::

*Interpretation notes.* The rate is a proportion between 0 and 1. Read it alongside
sign-in volume: a low success rate on very low volume may just be noise, while
the same rate on high volume is a real signal. In the weekly view, the first and
last weeks of the reporting window are usually partial (the window rarely starts
or ends on a Monday), so treat boundary weeks with caution when comparing trends.

This rate is computed over authentication *methods*, not sessions. When signing
in to CanadaLogin, IBM Verify records each method as its own event, so a mistyped
password is a failed event even when the user then succeeds and completes the
sign-in (see the [`auth_total_logins`](../catalog/ibm_verify.qmd#auth_total_logins)
caveat). The figure measures how often individual authentication steps succeed,
not how often a sign-in session ultimately completes. This differs from the
[per-application rate](sign-in-success-rate-by-application.qmd), which measures
credential validity against a relying party with no factors entered.
